title: Neolloydia macdowellii
taxonomy: Neolloydia macdowellii is a species of cactus in the genus Neolloydia. It belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae, although some sources may place it in the family Apocynaceae. Neolloydia macdowellii is further classified within the subfamily Pereskioideae. The species is a type of succulent, characterized by its fleshy stems and unique growth habits. It is native to the southwestern United States.

intro: Neolloydia macdowellii, commonly known as the macdowell's pincushion cactus, is a rare and unusual species of cactus native to the southwestern United States. It has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Neolloydia macdowellii include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
